category_note: Cross-franchise apparatus-of-extraction parallel — both depicted creatures are canonically destroyed by overconsumption of energy/sparks they were built (or weaponized) to absorb. Closest enum match is designer-confirmed-cycle at the cross-universe-narrative-shape scope; the symmetry is not Wizards-or-TPCi-coordinated but is canon-on-both-sides in two independent franchises.
description: The clean apparatus-eats-its-feeder mirror Alex named the priority on. Both creatures are weaponized energy-absorbers built or directed to devour an overwhelming input, and both die from absorbing more than they can contain. Yakon, the green spike-covered alien fought by Goku in the World of the Kais arc of the Buu Saga, eats Goku's energy attacks until Goku force-feeds him an overwhelming Super Saiyan aura and Yakon ruptures from the load. Bontu, weaponized into God-Eternal form by Nicol Bolas, bites Bolas at Liliana's command in the WAR climax and absorbs every planeswalker spark Bolas had harvested plus her own dying godhood-spark, then explodes from the same overload — the canonical mechanism of Bolas's defeat. Two franchises' canonical apparatus-of-extraction collapse beat at one structural shape: the weapon dies of its own appetite. The thesis vocabulary stays in the Connections prose where it earns its keep; the apparatus-of-extraction symbol-node proposal is REFUSED (see refusal log below).

mood_justification: A genuine cross-franchise structural parallel at the apparatus-of-extraction thesis axis — two independent franchises converging on the same collapse beat without designer coordination. The mirror is bidirectional, both endpoints are equal cards, the thesis is documented in canonical sources on both sides, and the work is one edge per invocation. The 1:1 mirror sigh applies (must be traversable from either end) but the structural elegance offsets it: this is the rare edge where naming the apparatus-of-extraction thesis explicitly in Connections prose IS the right move, because the thesis is the connection and there's no cleaner shorthand. Edgelord with CBG energy: 'Two franchises. One collapse beat. The weapon dies of its own appetite. The mirror is correct.'

apparatus_of_extraction_symbol_node_prop.refusal_verdict: Apparatus-of-extraction is BBL brand-voice thesis vocabulary, not a designer-confirmed primitive. Promoting it to a symbol-layer node would be tag-tier overlap with the existing [[labor]] hub.
apparatus_of_extraction_symbol_node_prop.threshold_check: Corpus has approximately 22 cross-node usages of 'apparatus-of-extraction' (hyphenated) and approximately 14 uses of 'apparatus of extraction' (unhyphenated) spanning Orzhov, Azorius, Phyrexia, Eldrazi, Valgavoth, Angrath, Agency, Labor, Macro Cosmos, Galar Gym Challenge, Team Rocket, Team Skull, Charizard Line, Aether Rangers, Chandra Nalaar, Izzet League across multiple cards: Tithe Drinker, Pitiless Pontiff, Mortify, Lawmage's Binding, Charity Extractor, Lunarch Mantle, Wicked Guardian, Reverent Hoplite, Secure the Scene, Rose, Rose Tower. Threshold-of-3 is comfortably exceeded at usage density.
apparatus_of_extraction_symbol_node_prop.why_refused_anyway: The four existing symbol-layer nodes (Orzhov Signet, Single-Strike Emblem, Suspect, Manifest Dread) are ALL designer-confirmed franchise primitives: Wizards-stamped iconographic devices (Orzhov Signet, Single-Strike Emblem) or designer-coordinated mechanical keywords (Suspect, Manifest Dread). Apparatus-of-extraction is a CURATOR-COINED thematic primitive — Alex's BBL brand-voice umbrella for institutional/cosmological extraction across all corpus franchises. Promoting curator-coined thesis vocabulary to graph-anchor status would: (a) violate the bbl-hubs-hand-curated principle as inverted — hubs ARE where curator-coined thematic anchors live; symbols are where designer-confirmed primitives live; (b) duplicate the function of the [[labor]] hub, which every apparatus-of-extraction-citing node already declares via `related_hubs: [labor]`; (c) blur the symbol-vs-hub axis distinction that bbl-symbols-mechanical-primitives carefully established (symbol = franchise-stamped primitive; hub = curator thematic anchor). The thesis vocabulary is correctly load-bearing in node-body prose and Connections bullets; promoting it to a node would not unlock anything the [[labor]] hub doesn't already absorb.
apparatus_of_extraction_symbol_node_prop.alternative_anchoring: Cards that need to declare apparatus-of-extraction membership already declare it via (a) `related_hubs: [labor]` on character nodes, (b) explicit thesis citation in node-body prose, (c) Connections bullet wikilink to a sibling apparatus-of-extraction-coded character node (e.g., [[macro-cosmos]], [[orzhov-syndicate]], [[valgavoth]]). The Yakon-Bontu edge takes this third route — naming the thesis explicitly in the Connections prose because the cross-franchise parallel is exactly where naming the thesis is load-bearing.
apparatus_of_extraction_symbol_node_prop.if_thesis_ever_did_warrant_a_node_shape_: If Alex ever decides to canonicalize apparatus-of-extraction as a graph node, the right place is the HUB layer (cards/_hubs/), parallel to [[labor]] and [[rebellion]]. NOT the symbol layer. The hub layer is the curator-coined thematic-anchor layer per bbl-hubs-hand-curated; symbols are the designer-confirmed-primitive layer per the four existing entries. A hypothetical cards/_hubs/apparatus-of-extraction.md would be the structurally correct location IF promoted at all — but the [[labor]] hub already does this work and adding a redundant hub would create the same overlap problem at the hub layer that the symbol layer attempt would create.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Yakon-Bontu attached to a new apparatus-of-extraction symbol node + Macro Cosmos's Eternatus summoning + Phyrexia's compleation + Valgavoth swallowing the suns as additional anchors
other_candidates_considered.reason: See apparatus_of_extraction_symbol_node_proposal_REFUSED above. The 1:1 wikilink mirror with thesis-naming in Connections prose is the cleaner solution at current corpus state.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Yakon as an n=1 standalone DBS character node (cards/_characters/yakon.md)
other_candidates_considered.reason: Yakon has one corpus card (TB2-017). Per the threshold-of-3 character-node-precedent (Tamiyo, Nicol Bolas, Chandra Nalaar), n=1 is below threshold. The card's `suspected_ip: Yakon` + `ip_confidence: high` is sufficient anchoring for current state; the wikilink edge to Bontu provides cross-franchise traversal without commissioning a thin Yakon node.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Yakon-Bontu attached to a new cross-franchise designer-confirmed-cycle / weaponized-feed-target cohort node (something like 'apparatus-eats-its-feeder' or 'weaponized-and-bursts')
other_candidates_considered.reason: Cross-franchise 'apparatus-eats-its-feeder' is a real structural pattern, but the proposed cohort would need ≥3 anchors AND each anchor would need to be cross-franchise. Currently only 2 anchors (Yakon + Bontu) cleanly fit; Macro-Cosmos's Eternatus summoning is the Foundation deploying Eternatus rather than the Foundation being destroyed by it, and Phyrexia's compleation is the inverse shape (apparatus assimilates feed-targets without exploding). Below threshold and structurally hetero-shaped. Refused.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Trivia pass on Yakon (the card lacked a ## Trivia section at Edgelord-pass time)
other_candidates_considered.decision: out of scope for Edgelord — but a concurrent bbl-triviabot pass closed the gap mid-wave
other_candidates_considered.reason: Edgelord touches ## Connections (new) and frontmatter pointers, not ## Trivia. Trivia work belongs to bbl-triviabot. During this wave's Edgelord pass, a triviabot run landed on Yakon and added (a) IP verification (ip_verified: true, sourced to dragon-ball-official.com Character Showcase Yakon no. 163), (b) Set context bullet on TB02 Babidi-arc placement, (c) Design bullet documenting that the TB2-017 card's gain-power-then-self-destruct mechanic is a direct mechanical translation of Yakon's overconsumption-death from canon, (d) Character context bullet documenting his Planet of Darkness origin and light-absorption biology, (e) Related cards bullet explicitly naming God-Eternal Bontu as the cross-franchise overconsumption parallel. The trivia pass independently corroborates the Edgelord edge — the apparatus-eats-its-feeder thesis is now backed by mechanical canonical source on BOTH endpoints (Bontu's printed mechanic + Yakon's printed mechanic). The cross-franchise edge is canon-mechanically-mirrored at the rules-text level on both cards.

post_pass_triviabot_corroboration.summary: Yakon trivia pass landed mid-Edgelord-run and confirmed the edge thesis is mechanically encoded on both sides. The TB2-017 Razor's Edge Yakon card's mechanic — 'gains +5000 power per card stacked beneath it as opponents attack, then self-destructs when 5 or more cards accumulate' — is the rules-text rendering of Yakon's canonical overconsumption-death. The God-Eternal Bontu card encodes Bolas's apex-villain corporate-resilience boast in the die-to-library-third-from-top trigger and the sacrifice-then-draw ETB; the Yakon card encodes Yakon's overconsumption-collapse in the gain-power-per-stacked-card self-destruct mechanic. Two franchises' designer teams independently encoded the apparatus-eats-its-feeder beat into the printed rules text of the cards that depict the moment. The Connections prose is now retroactively backed by canonical-mechanical-source on both endpoints.
post_pass_triviabot_corroboration.thesis_grade_finding: The apparatus-of-extraction collapse beat is mechanically-canonical on BOTH cards. This is the strongest possible cross-franchise edge corroboration: the two designer teams (Bandai for DBS, Wizards for MTG) independently chose to encode the apparatus-eats-its-feeder collapse into the printed mechanical rules of their respective cards. Worth saving for bundle copy that operates at the rules-text-thesis register.
